description: An annual bunching radish featuring elongated red roots with a distinct white tip. It is prized for its crisp texture and mild flavor. Extremely fast-growing, making it perfect for filling small gaps in the garden.

characteristics.hardiness: H2 (Tolerant of low temperatures but will not survive being frozen)
characteristics.waterNeeds: high and THEN info: Regular — keep soil moist to delay bolting
characteristics.soilType: Moist but well-drained; Chalk, Clay, Loam, or Sand

pinchOutTops.info: Not typically required for this plant.

care.sowOutdoors: Sow seeds 1cm deep, 2–3cm apart, directly into containers from late March. Keep moist. Seedlings appear in 3–5 days.
care.plantOut: No transplanting needed — sow directly where they'll grow. Radishes don't like being moved.
care.watering: Ensure the soil remains consistently moist to prevent the roots from becoming woody or 'bolting' (flowering prematurely).
care.feeding: No feeding needed — radishes grow too fast to benefit from it.
care.harvest: Pull up when the root is about 2–3cm across (check by brushing soil away from the top). Typically ready in 21–28 days.
care.overwintering: Not applicable — sow winter radish varieties (mooli) in late summer for autumn harvest.

propagation.seedDescription: Small, round, reddish-brown seeds about 3mm across. Very easy to handle and sow. Stay viable for 4+ years.

deadheading.notes: Not applicable — harvest before flowering. If radishes bolt and flower, the roots become inedible, but the flowers are edible and tasty in salads.

seedSaving.method: Let a few radishes bolt and flower. After flowering, seed pods form — wait until they turn brown and dry, then pick and crack open to collect seeds.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Long, pointed seed pods that rattle when dry. Each pod contains several round seeds.

commonProblems: Flea beetles (which leave small holes in the leaves)
Woody/pithy roots — left in the ground too long. Harvest promptly when ready.
funFact: Extremely versatile in the kitchen—great for eating raw, pickling, or even cooking with butter and garlic!
